_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of methods, tools, sources, and techniques used to research, integrate and summarize all-source information pertaining to target.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data lineage concepts and documentation methods, including end-to-end lineage from source to consumption, transformation-level lineage, and impact analysis use cases.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.536), DG-K-011 (DG, cosine: 0.526).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.1/10 (raw 0.607). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, methods, source]. DCWF-only terms [information, integrate, pertaining, research, sources, summarize] vs WIDAI-only terms [analysis, cases, concepts, consumption, data, documentation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.867 (P=0.881, R=0.854). P exceeds R by 0.027 — the DCWF element's 18 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). "Knowledge of methods, tools, sources, and techniques used to research, integrate" and "Knowledge of data lineage concepts and documentation methods, including end-to-e" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.545 < STS 0.607 (gap -0.062). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [information, integrate, pertaining] and [analysis, cases, concepts] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 19.4%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, methods, source]. DCWF-only: [information, integrate, pertaining, research, sources, summarize]. WIDAI-only: [analysis, cases, concepts, consumption, data, documentation].
